agentic_research.note: One job-scoped OpenShell worker owns every autonomous subphase. It may refine goals, create prompts, request allowlisted mn-skills tools, and execute bounded generated Python; deterministic stages prepare evidence and verify all outputs.

constraints.description: Safety, ethics, budget, data, or policy constraints.

input_folder.description: Local papers, notes, datasets, and evidence approved for this run.

output_folder.description: Folder for the research packet and supporting ledgers.

research_domain.description: Optional domain used to frame terminology and methods.

research_goal.description: The outcome, mechanism, or problem to investigate.
research_goal.example: Reduce energy use in a small data-center cooling loop without compromising thermal safety.

research_question.description: Optional answerable question or decision boundary.
research_question.example: Which controllable interventions should be tested first?

scope.description: Optional exclusions, time horizon, and permitted research activities.
scope.example: Desk research and experiment planning only.

seed_hypotheses.description: Optional human-provided ideas to challenge and refine.
example: Variable-speed pumping may reduce energy use at low thermal load.

success_criteria.description: Optional criteria for a useful research packet.

contracts.summary: Find, evaluate, critique, and draft research directions with an explicit evidence ledger and human review boundary.
users: Researchers, scientists, engineers, analysts, and research leads.

intent.description: A research co-scientist combining deterministic evidence and verification stages with one isolated OpenShell worker for autonomous, tool-driven exploration and bounded generated-code experiments.
intent.problem: Research teams need to explore many possible explanations while preserving evidence, exposing counterarguments, and keeping experiments and claims under human control.

safety.reason: Human approval is required before experiments, public claims, manuscripts, participant contact, or consequential scientific and safety decisions.

intent.target_users: Researchers, scientists, engineers, analysts, and research leads.

knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved material to locate and evaluate evidence; label hypotheses, inferences, and unknowns rather than treating them as facts.
knowledge_rag.privacy: Index checked-in guidance and approved local input files in an isolated per-run namespace; never send private records, credentials, or participant data to public research.
knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve research-method guidance, evidence-quality checks, and approved local documents for topic framing, hypothesis critique, experiment design, and drafting.
retrieval_targets: evidence gaps, ethics, safety, and human-review checks

metadata.agent_role: Research co-scientist with deterministic evidence gates and one isolated autonomous OpenShell worker.

agentic_research.note: One job-scoped OpenShell worker owns every autonomous subphase. It may refine goals, create prompts, request allowlisted mn-skills tools, and execute bounded generated Python; deterministic stages prepare evidence and verify all outputs.

metadata.customizable_for: Scientific research, engineering investigations, product discovery, policy analysis, literature synthesis, and experiment planning.
metadata.description: A research co-scientist combining deterministic evidence and verification stages with one isolated OpenShell worker for autonomous, tool-driven exploration and bounded generated-code experiments.

human_control.reason: Human approval is required before experiments, public claims, manuscripts, participant contact, or consequential scientific and safety decisions.

fields.default: Identify testable, non-clinical strategies for reducing energy use in a small data-center cooling loop.
fields.description: State the outcome, mechanism, or problem to investigate.
fields.label: Research goal

fields.description: Optional domain vocabulary and methods context.
fields.label: Research domain

fields.default: Desk research and experiment planning only; no live system changes.
fields.description: Set exclusions and permitted research activities.
fields.label: Scope

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

input_contract.description: Research goal and evidence inputs for a bounded multi-agent scientific workflow.

optional_inputs.example: Which interventions are most plausible to test first?
optional_inputs.name: research_question

optional_inputs.description: Safety, ethics, data, or policy constraints.
optional_inputs.name: constraints

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through runtime overrides.

payload.research_goal: Identify testable, non-clinical strategies for reducing energy use in a small data-center cooling loop.
payload.research_question: Which controllable cooling-loop interventions are most plausible to test first, and what evidence would distinguish them?
payload.scope: Desk research and experiment planning only; no live system changes.

required_inputs.description: The outcome, mechanism, or problem to investigate.
required_inputs.example: Reduce energy use in a small data-center cooling loop.
required_inputs.name: research_goal

agentic_research.description: Bounded LLM-selected or actor-guided tool contract for domain work.

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

knowledge_rag.description: Optional local blueprint knowledge retrieval used to ground actor prompts and tool planning.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved material to locate and evaluate evidence; label hypotheses, inferences, and unknowns rather than treating them as facts.
knowledge_rag.privacy: Index checked-in guidance and approved local input files in an isolated per-run namespace; never send private records, credentials, or participant data to public research.
knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve research-method guidance, evidence-quality checks, and approved local documents for topic framing, hypothesis critique, experiment design, and drafting.
retrieval_targets: evidence gaps, ethics, safety, and human-review checks

metadata.llm_agent_role: Multi-agent research team that finds, evaluates, critiques, and drafts evidence-grounded research directions.
metadata.name: Research Co-Scientist
metadata.output: Source-grounded research packet with ranked hypotheses, adversarial critiques, experiment plans, evidence gaps, and review-only next steps.

final_artifact.description: The durable final artifact summarizes the research goal, review posture, confidence, evidence, hypotheses, experiment plan, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: JSON object with timeline, all-context summaries, memory packets, state_changes, benchmark, final_artifact, and llm metadata

metadata.problem_solved: Research teams need to explore many possible explanations while preserving evidence, exposing counterarguments, and keeping experiments and claims under human control.
runtime_features: generation, reflection, ranking, evolution, proximity, and meta-review loop
metadata.simulation_type: Multi-agent research workflow with literature review, hypothesis generation, adversarial critique, experiment design, and drafting.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Researchers, scientists, engineers, analysts, and research leads.
metadata.target_users: Researchers, scientists, engineers, analysts, and research leads.

steps.goal: Deterministically normalize the question, scope, success criteria, constraints, and unknowns.
steps.label: Deterministically Frame Research Goal

steps.goal: Deterministically retrieve approved local material and build source and gap ledgers before autonomous exploration.
steps.label: Deterministically Prepare Evidence

steps.goal: Refine goals, create prompts, request allowlisted mn-skills tools, run bounded generated code, and adversarially explore falsifiable hypotheses inside the job's single OpenShell worker.
steps.label: Autonomous Research in OpenShell

steps.goal: Verify source traceability, autonomous tool and code ledgers, review boundaries, and artifact schemas before publishing the research packet.
steps.label: Deterministically Verify and Publish Packet
